spec_match.rationale: Uses position-try-fallbacks plus position-try-options: most-scroll-position. The latter is the v135 keyword. Verify against the current css-anchor-position-1 spec whether both names apply.

interactive.rationale: Real scrollbox with anchor and popover; toggle adds/removes .remember class controlling position-try-options.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Single scroller toggle for most-scroll-position — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Feature-detect only probes anchor-name, not position-try-options: most-scroll-position specifically.

openQuestions.title: Verify position-try-options vs position-try-fallbacks naming
openQuestions.detail: Confirm against the current spec which name Chrome 135 accepts; update all four sibling concepts.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the feature-detect probe
openQuestions.detail: Add CSS.supports("position-try-options", "most-scroll-position") so the pill reflects the v135-specific feature.

openQuestions.title: Show measured flip count
openQuestions.detail: Add a counter logging each time the popover flips so the ON/OFF delta is quantified.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate position-area integration
openQuestions.detail: Spec note mentions sizing via position-area. This concept doesn't touch it. Add a follow-up combining remembered offset with position-area sizing.

summary: Clean single-toggle demo, but the syntax may be drifting from the current spec. Verify against Chrome 135 actuals.
